sources.edition: Musica Antiqua Bohemica, ed. V. Sykora, Prague, 1954
sources.notes: Used only to resolve hard-to-read noteheads and rests in the Benda scan. Its editorial dynamics, phrasing, fingering, pedaling, and articulations were excluded, and every selected event was rechecked against the 1757 authority. The PDF is not copied into this worktree or proposed for publication. Hash and IMSLP identifier are retained so the exact operator aid is auditable if the separate main checkout is cleaned.

sources.edition: Louise Farrenc, Le Tresor des pianistes, Paris, 1861
sources.notes: Retained as an orientation record only. It was not an authority for any selected event or expression and is not copied into this worktree or proposed for publication. Hash and IMSLP identifier are retained so the exact operator file can be distinguished from the authority and legibility aids.

Sonata No. 2 in G begins within scan pages 14-19; movement iii is on scan pages 18-19 (plates 11-12). This first edition is the content and expression authority. The MAB 1954 edition was used for note/rest legibility only; its editorial dynamics, phrasing, fingering, pedaling, and articulations were excluded. The embedded PDF image and pinned IIIF service both expose a 1000 x 707 maximum.

Vienna ONB copy. Independently fetched twice on 2026-07-18 (operator session and worktree agent); byte-identical. Movement iii is on PDF pages 16-17 (plates 13-14). The 1742 print is the expression authority; Nagel 1927 and the C. P. E. Bach Complete Works were consulted for notehead legibility only. Manual traversal resolves the movement at 78 measures.

sources.edition: Longman and Broderip early print (BnF copy, ark btv1b9010256n)
sources.notes: Fetched twice independently on 2026-07-18 (operator Downloads and mirror); byte-identical. This BnF scan remains the notation and expression authority. A corrected traversal gives 119 measures (45 + 46 + 28), part 2 at m. 46, and the thematic pickup at m. 70 onset 3 qn into the first full thematic bar at m. 71; fixed windows are W2 64-69, W3 70-75, and W4 76-81. Schema 3.1.0 resolves the former textual dim./dimi: representation blocker. Only actual mm. 69-71 have converged to JSON-safe events; the other 24 evidence measures still require an authority-based event double pass. No CASE-Z3F9 dossier has been promoted.

sources.citation: Andrew Brownell, The English Piano in the Classical Period: Its Music, Performers, and Influences (DMA thesis, City University London, 2010), pp. 71-72, Example 3.8.
sources.notes: Example 3.8 is captioned Sonata Op. 10 No. 3, I, mm. 67-74 and Copied from Torricella, Vienna, 1783. The excerpt matches the BnF sequence and independently resolves the five-measure numbering error, but it is a partial copied excerpt rather than the complete Torricella source. It is a coordinate and concordance witness only and never supersedes the BnF authority for selected notes or expression. Do not republish the excerpt image without separate rights review.
